scenario.name: Mission 2: Ransomed Trust - Hospital Backup Server
scenario.author: Z. Cliffe Schreuders
scenario.description:
# Mission 2: Ransomed Trust
St. Catherine's Regional Medical Center has been hit by ransomware. Forty-seven patients on life support systems are at risk. Ghost has encrypted critical hospital systems and is demanding $87,000 in Bitcoin.
You have been deployed to investigate the attack and restore hospital operations. Intelligence suggests Ghost infiltrated the network weeks ago via an insider — codename "Asset #47" — who planted equipment and exploited an unpatched ProFTPD vulnerability on the hospital's backup server.
## Your Objectives
1. **Gain access to the hospital backup server** - The server is running a vulnerable ProFTPD service 2. **Locate Ghost's operational files** - Evidence of the infiltration is hidden on the system 3. **Recover the offline backup keys** - These keys can decrypt patient data without paying the ransom 4. **Identify the insider** - Find evidence linking Asset #47 to the attack 5. **Trace ENTROPY's attack methodology** - Understand how Ghost penetrated hospital security
## Investigation Brief
**IT Administrator**: Marcus Webb warned hospital management about the ProFTPD vulnerability 6 months ago. His warnings were ignored. Ghost is now using him as a scapegoat.
**Network Bridge**: Asset #47 planted a wireless network bridge in the IT department, providing Ghost remote access to the backup server.
**Equipment Manifest**: Ghost's operational files should reveal the full scope of the infiltration, including details about Asset #47, the equipment deployed, and the hospital emergency storage safe PIN.
**Database Backup**: The server contains encrypted patient database backups. Finding these will reveal the path to Ghost's operational logs and prove Marcus Webb's innocence.
## Technical Challenge Path
You will need to: - Exploit the ProFTPD backdoor (CVE-2010-4652) - Escalate privileges to access restricted files - Decode Ghost's obfuscated operational files - Connect technical evidence to physical game elements (PIN cracker device, guard identity)
## Narrative Payoff
Successfully rooting this server will: - Prove Marcus Webb was warning about vulnerabilities (vindicating him) - Reveal Asset #47's identity as the hospital security guard - Expose the PIN (4729) needed to open the emergency storage safe - Provide evidence of Ghost's infiltration methodology - Connect the VM challenge to the physical escape room investigation
Ghost doesn't know you're coming. Make it count.

input.value:
From: Marcus Webb <mwebb@stcatherines.hospital> To: Hospital IT Committee <itcommittee@stcatherines.hospital> Date: November 12, 2025 Subject: URGENT: ProFTPD Vulnerability Requires Immediate Patching
Dear Committee Members,
I am writing to formally document a CRITICAL security vulnerability on our backup server (backup01.stcatherines.local).
VULNERABILITY DETAILS: - Service: ProFTPD 1.3.3c - CVE: CVE-2010-4652 (Backdoor in distribution) - CVSS Score: 10.0 (CRITICAL) - Exploit Status: Public exploits available
RISK ASSESSMENT: This backdoor allows UNAUTHENTICATED REMOTE ROOT ACCESS. An attacker could: - Access all patient database backups - Steal offline recovery keys - Plant ransomware or malware - Exfiltrate sensitive medical records
RECOMMENDATION: IMMEDIATE patching to ProFTPD 1.3.4 or later is required. Estimated downtime: 30 minutes during maintenance window.
This is my THIRD WARNING about this vulnerability. Previous warnings: August 2025, September 2025
I take no responsibility for breaches resulting from continued inaction.
Regards, Marcus Webb Senior IT Administrator St. Catherine's Regional Medical Center

ST. CATHERINE'S HOSPITAL - BACKUP PROCEDURES
OFFLINE BACKUP ENCRYPTION KEYS Location: Emergency Storage Safe (Physical Security) Safe Model: Sargent & Greenleaf 6120 Access: PIN-locked (4-digit code) PIN Location: Restricted (IT Director only)
ONLINE RECOVERY KEYS Location: /var/backups/hospital_db/ Encryption: AES-256 Access: Root user only
RECOVERY PROCEDURE: 1. Retrieve offline backup keys from emergency storage safe 2. Combine with online recovery keys from backup server 3. Use combined keys to decrypt patient database backups 4. Restore to production systems
NOTE: Both key sets are required for full recovery. Marcus Webb documented this procedure after management refused to implement proper cloud backup solutions.
For investigation of suspected intrusion, check: /root/.ghost_ops/ directory (if it exists - indicates compromise)

OPERATION RANSOMED TRUST - EQUIPMENT DEPLOYMENT LOG ====================================================
INFILTRATION TIMELINE: Week 1: Network bridge deployment Week 3: Emergency storage reconnaissance Week 6: ProFTPD exploit execution
ASSET DETAILS: ------------- Asset ID: #47 Cover: Hospital Security Guard (Night Shift) Handler: Ghost Payment: $8,700 (10% of projected ransom) Status: ACTIVE
EQUIPMENT ISSUED: ---------------- 1. Wireless Network Bridge (Netgear R6400) - Serial: WNB-2024-0847-A - Location: IT Department, hidden behind server rack - Purpose: Remote access foothold - Status: Deployed Week 1, operational
2. PIN Cracker Device v2.4 - Serial: RI-2024-0847 - Location: Server Room, maintenance cabinet (Rack 3) - Purpose: Emergency storage safe access (reconnaissance) - Target PIN discovered: 4729 - Status: Concealed Week 6, ready for recovery
3. ProFTPD Exploit Payload (CVE-2010-4652) - Deployment: Week 6 via network bridge - Execution: Successful - Ransom note delivered: Confirmed
RECOVERY INSTRUCTIONS: --------------------- Equipment Location: Server Maintenance Cabinet - Rack 3, bottom shelf - Behind power supply stack - Access code: Asset47
Emergency Safe PIN (if needed): 4729 Payment wallet: bc1q7cyrfmckn2ewz5m78ts6jt9lcfhfwcvzefm27k
OPERATIONAL SECURITY: -------------------- - Asset #47 has plausible deniability (legitimate security role) - Equipment serials sanitized (untraceable to ENTROPY) - Payment routed through 3 BTC mixers - If Asset compromised: DENY ALL KNOWLEDGE
-- Ghost, Ransomware Incorporated

ST. CATHERINE'S HOSPITAL - BACKUP SERVER =========================================
FTP Service: ProFTPD 1.3.3c Status: ACTIVE Anonymous Access: READ-ONLY
Available Backups: - /backups/public/system_configs/ (public) - /backups/public/application_logs/ (public)
RESTRICTED AREAS (requires authentication): - /var/backups/hospital_db/ (encrypted patient data) - /root/.ghost_ops/ (UNAUTHORIZED - investigate if found)
For authorized access, contact: Marcus Webb (mwebb@stcatherines.hospital)
System Administrator: Marcus Webb Last Security Audit: OVERDUE (6 months) Known Vulnerabilities: UNPATCHED (management decision)

ASSET #47 OPERATIONAL INSTRUCTIONS (ENCODED FOR SECURITY) ===================================
Your role is CRITICAL to Operation Ransomed Trust.
PHASE 1 - RECONNAISSANCE (Weeks 1-4): - Plant network bridge in IT room (COMPLETE) - Map security systems and access points (COMPLETE) - Identify high-value targets (COMPLETE) - Scout emergency storage layout (COMPLETE)
PHASE 2 - EXECUTION (Week 6): - Verify network bridge still operational - Confirm backup server vulnerability unpatched - Report final go/no-go status - Execute ProFTPD exploit remotely - Monitor hospital response - Maintain cover as legitimate security
PHASE 3 - POST-ATTACK (Current): - Act as eyes on the ground - Report any investigation attempts - Prevent evidence discovery if possible - DO NOT interfere with ransom payment process - Maintain security guard role
PAYMENT SCHEDULE: - Phase 1 completion: $3,000 (PAID) - Phase 2 completion: $2,500 (PAID) - Equipment deployment: $2,000 (PAID) - Final payment: $1,200 (on ransom collection) Total: $8,700
Your daughter's medical bills will be covered regardless. Ghost keeps his promises.
-- R.I. (Ransomware Incorporated)
